mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in monthly employment levels signals an imminent economic contraction, causing risk-off sentiment to drive equity sell-offs within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 500 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in over 60% of instances where PAYEMS drops >500k, indicating the signal fails to capture forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and retail sales reveals aggregate demand strength; when both series rise simultaneously, consumer spending momentum supports corporate revenue growth, lifting equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST and RSAFS moving in the same direction) shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic activity and corporate capacity utilization; sustained expansion in INDPRO drives earnings revisions upward, producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a 2.0% threshold, the market prices in tighter financial conditions and higher borrowing costs, which compress equity multiples and depress forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances following a rate crossing above 2.0%, invalidating the negative directionality.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, providing a structural tailwind for equity valuations over a three-month period.
failure_condition: Unemployment declines are followed by negative or flat 60-day forward returns in over 50% of observations, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in housing starts and real retail sales indicate a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate profit margins, triggering equity drawdowns within a one-month window.
failure_condition: Equity markets do not decline when both HOUST and RSAFS fall concurrently, or the joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production relative to historical trends signals supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints, which compresses corporate margins and leads to negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive or statistically neutral when INDPRO growth falls below its 3-month moving average, refuting the negative directional claim.
